closure_rule: Each weekly review action must be consumed before new greenfield weekly review conclusions are accepted.

prior_actions.action: Keep priority on completing the information-brain mainline before expanding knowledge-brain or new evolution scope.
evidence: CURRENT_OPERATIONS names source_intelligence_quality_resumption as the active mainline.
CURRENT_MAINLINE_CONTROL requires the next mainline task to remain a bounded source-intelligence quality packet.
Dirty worktree classification still shows large flywheel_readiness and evolution_ui groups, so support work pressure remains active.
prior_actions.next: Keep source intelligence as the only active mainline implementation lane; park new knowledge/evolution expansion unless it is a review gate blocker.

prior_actions.action: Treat self-evolution as a support verifier, not the primary product track.
evidence: CURRENT_MAINLINE_CONTROL lists flywheel and visual control work as separate lanes, not the mainline.
The current dirty worktree still contains substantial flywheel_readiness and evolution_ui entries.
prior_actions.next: Allow flywheel/evolution only as support or UI lanes; require reinforced governance before any runtime-truth, scheduler, persistence, or promotion semantics work.

prior_actions.action: Freeze or sharply limit new research-track surface area until internal fetch persistence, feed DB-read cutover, and Phase 2 acceptance are complete.
evidence: The current controller surface has replaced the older feed fact-store Phase 2 wording with source_intelligence_quality_resumption.
The underlying constraint remains valid: do not expand research/docs surface while source intelligence and worktree governance are not closed.
prior_actions.next: Use the updated rule: no new durable research docs unless they directly serve active source-intelligence mainline, review absorption, or operations control.

prior_actions.action: Start a documentation consolidation pass because current docs are numerous and partially stale relative to code and tests.
evidence: CURRENT_OPERATIONS, CURRENT_MAINLINE_CONTROL, CURRENT_ACTIVE_ARTIFACTS, and CURRENT_REVIEW_QUEUE were created as controller-facing active surfaces.
Archive and review identity index artifacts exist.
Dirty worktree classification still reports 218 entries across 9 groups, so consolidation is not closed.
prior_actions.next: Continue lane-specific scoped cleanup only; do not run broad archive, deletion, movement, or mixed PR operations from the dirty worktree.

prior_actions.action: Make weekly project review a closed-loop control instead of a passive summary.
evidence: This ledger is the first controller-curated action ledger.
The automation prompt still needs to require prior-action closure on every run.
prior_actions.next: Update the weekly automation prompt to start with closure on this ledger and to write status for every prior action.

prior_actions.action: Treat unresolved weekly review items as carry-over constraints, not rediscovered findings.
evidence: This ledger assigns completed, partial, not_started, or superseded status to prior actions.
CURRENT_OPERATIONS is updated to require review consumption before greenfield review.
prior_actions.next: Future weekly reviews must update this ledger before introducing new conclusions.

prior_actions.action: Persist a machine-readable action ledger or closure status per action.
evidence: docs/CURRENT_WEEKLY_REVIEW_ACTION_LEDGER.json exists as the active weekly review action ledger.
prior_actions.next: Keep the ledger compact and controller-curated; do not let it become a second historical doc archive.

current_priority_changes: Source intelligence quality remains the only active mainline implementation priority.
GitHub/Codex PR review becomes the default L2 review gate for scoped implementation promotion.
UI control surface remains an independent lane and should move through GitHub PRs for cross-IDE collaboration.
Weekly review outputs must create or update explicit action ledger entries before they are considered consumed.
Tooling incidents that break standard operations, such as rg execution failure, must be fixed before falling back to lower-quality workflows.

escalations.reason: Current classifier output reports 218 entries across 9 groups.
escalations.required_action: No broad commit, broad push, or mixed PR; use scoped lane-specific PR prep only.

escalations.item: Review automation is not closed-loop enough
escalations.reason: Automation memory notes strategic conclusions but previously lacked action-ledger persistence.
escalations.required_action: Update automation prompt and require ledger closure before new conclusions.

escalations.reason: Bundled WindowsApps rg was present in PATH but could not execute with Access denied.
escalations.required_action: Use user-installed ripgrep via WinGet Links and treat future key-tool failures as incidents.

escalations.reason: Antigravity UI line cannot depend on manual context transfer.
escalations.required_action: Move UI lane through GitHub issue/branch/PR/review comments with static-truth boundaries in the PR description.

updated_next_actions.action: Update weekly project review automation prompt so it starts with prior-action closure and updates this ledger.
Prepare scoped GitHub/Codex L2 PR candidate for the accepted GitHub signal relation hints source-intelligence slice.
Define Antigravity UI collaboration through GitHub PR protocol, with allowed files limited to control-surface UI and no backend runtime truth changes.
Write a bounded adapter design packet for Claude/qoderlic, Gemini CLI, and GitHub Codex review surfaces.
